name: RENAL INFARCTION
definition: dislodgment of multiple atheromatous emboli from the aorta into renal circulation → below disseminated process involving most of the interlobar + arcuate arteries causing uniform

demographics.Path: all elements of kidney atrophied with replacement by interstitial fibrosis
demographics.Histo: thickening + hyalinization of afferent arterioles, proliferative endarteritis,

distribution: absent / diminished nephrogram with cortical rim enhancement, rarely striations
discriminating_features: normal / large kidney with smooth contour
normal / attenuated collecting system, often only opacified by retrograde pyelography
absent / diminished nephrogram with cortical rim enhancement, rarely striations

general: normal / large kidney with smooth contour
normal / attenuated collecting system, often only opacified by retrograde pyelography
absent / diminished nephrogram with cortical rim enhancement, rarely striations
CT: edematous enlargement of kidney (with large infarct)
"cortical rim" sign within several days after global infarction
nonperfused area corresponding to vascular division
"cortical rim" sign (subacute) = thin rim of preserved subcapsular perfusion ← capsular
US: focal attenuation of collecting system ← tissue swelling
focally absent nephrogram (triangular with base at cortex)
focally wasted parenchyma with NORMAL interpapillary line (portion of lobe / whole
normal / small kidney with smooth contour
increased size of renal sinus echoes ← fatty replacement
radiographic appearance similar to arteriosclerotic kidney
ANGIO: late visualization of renal arteries on abdominal aortogram
normal / small kidneys with smooth contour or shallow depressions
smooth contour with random shallow contour depressions ← infarctions
normal / effaced collecting system ← fat proliferation
increased pelvic radiolucency (vicarious sinus fat proliferation)

clinical.Rx: thrombolytic therapy, supportive hemodialysis, transcatheter thrombembolectomy,
